pattern: Union-Find over rows and columns: merging stones that share a row or column lets you compute connected components, and the answer is stones minus component count.
hints: Two stones are in the same connected component if they share a row or column, even indirectly through a chain of shared rows/columns.
Use Union-Find with row IDs and column IDs (offset to distinguish them) as nodes; union each stone's row and column together.
The maximum removable stones equals total stones minus the number of distinct connected components.

edge_inputs.description: chain: row link then column link connects all three
two separate components of equal size

stress.description: random stones on 10000x10000 grid, up to 1000 stones
